title: GC integration for refc binaries — ProcBin sweep and virtual binary heap
purpose: B-072 added a GC stub that copies ProcBin terms and increments Arc ref counts. But it doesn't decrement ref counts when ProcBins become unreachable, and it doesn't account for off-heap binary memory in GC scheduling decisions. Without proper ref count management, SharedBinary data leaks. Without virtual binary heap accounting, a process holding 100MB of refc binary data but only 233 words of heap terms never triggers GC — the GC only sees the small heap.
task: Implement proper ProcBin lifecycle in the GC: decrement ref counts for unreachable ProcBins, add virtual binary heap accounting to GC trigger decisions, and sweep unreferenced SharedBinary data.

requirements.title: ProcBin ref count decrement on collection
requirements.spec: When a ProcBin is unreachable (not copied during GC), its SharedBinary Arc must be decremented. If the ref count reaches zero, the off-heap data is freed.
acceptance: After minor GC, unreachable ProcBins in young space have their Arc decremented
After major GC, unreachable ProcBins in both spaces have their Arc decremented
When Arc strong_count reaches 0, the SharedBinary Vec is deallocated
Test: create ProcBin, make it unreachable, trigger GC — SharedBinary is freed (verify via Arc::strong_count on a clone)
Test: create ProcBin shared between parent ref and heap, make heap ref unreachable, trigger GC — SharedBinary survives (ref count still > 0)

requirements.title: Virtual binary heap accounting
requirements.spec: Add a `virtual_binary_heap` counter to the Process that tracks the total byte size of off-heap binaries referenced by the process. This counter influences GC scheduling decisions.
acceptance: Process gains `virtual_binary_heap: usize` field (bytes of off-heap binary data)
Creating a ProcBin increments virtual_binary_heap by the SharedBinary's byte length
GC decrements virtual_binary_heap for unreachable ProcBins
The GC trigger check considers `heap_used + virtual_binary_heap` against the threshold, not just heap_used
Test: process with small heap but 1MB of refc binaries triggers GC sooner than a process with same heap but no binaries

requirements.title: ProcBin copy increments ref count
requirements.spec: When GC copies a ProcBin from young to old space (or during major compaction), the Arc ref count must be incremented to reflect the new copy.
acceptance: copy_young_term / copy_any_term for ProcBin increments Arc::clone (ref count +1)
After copy, the old ProcBin's ref count is decremented (R1) and the new copy's is correct
Net effect: ref count stays the same if the ProcBin survives collection
Test: ProcBin survives minor GC — ref count unchanged after collection

requirements.title: Process exit decrements all ProcBin refs
requirements.spec: When a process exits, all ProcBins on its heap must have their Arc ref counts decremented. This ensures SharedBinary data is freed when the last process referencing it exits.
acceptance: Process cleanup (deallocation) walks the heap and decrements Arc for every ProcBin found
SharedBinaries with no remaining references are freed
Test: spawn process, send it a large binary (ProcBin), process exits — SharedBinary freed if no other references

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement a separate binary GC pass — integrate into the existing minor/major collection cycle
SHALL NOT change the GC algorithm (generational copying) — only add ref count bookkeeping
SHALL NOT implement binary defragmentation or compaction
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
